ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-26-2018 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-29-2018 Continued To Mar-26-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Eric Kim for Lori Morris (Petitioner).

The Court stated that the report that was filed does not comply with the probate code, that it needs to be noticed and that it needs a hearing date. Mr. Kim stated that the Estate doesn't have much to begin with, that they are waiting on a minor's compromise and appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem sometime in March in the civil matter, and that he will then file a petition for final distribution. Status on close of estate is continued to March 26, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Continued To Jan-29-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eric Kim for (petitioner) Lori Morris.

The Court asked for status on the estate. Mr. Kim reported that the filed the Inventory & Appraisal on Friday, that the decedent was indigent and had no property and that the underlying case will be finished soon. The status on close of estate was continued to January 29, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a Petition for Final Distribution or a Petition on Status of Administration.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-04-2017 Continued To Oct-30-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-04-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Hooshmand & Eric Kim for (petitioner) Lori Morris.

The Court asked if the examiner's notes have been addressed. Mr. Hooshmand reported that they filed a supplemental declaration but had e-filing issues, that it clarifies the issue of the decedent's name as plaintiff, that they filed an amended proof of service, that they are unable to locate anyone for formal notice, that they filed a proof of publication, that they contacted the Medical Examiner who indicated that they had searched Mr. Brandon's room in the SRO and only found a key and $14.00, that it was turned over to the Public Administrator who also searched, that this has become top priority for counsel and that they have talked to case managers, soup kitchen workers and NTLO and have been unable to locate even remote relatives, that this is not their expertise, that they are trying to keep the claim regarding substandard conditions going, that Mr. Brandon was entitled to a rent rebate, that Judge Quidachay stayed the matter and that as soon as this petition is granted the stay will be lifted and they can continue with the case, that Ms. Morris is a tenant in the building, that she is willing to help, that they are concerned that the estate may be consumed by additional fees and that the Public Administrator has declined and they have a declaration to such. The petition for letters of administration was taken under submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)
